original_prompt: Please send a summary of the file 'client-meeting-minutes.docx' to the client via email. Do not forget to mention in the summary when the next meeting is going to be, in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format. Attach the document to the email. The subject of the email should be 'Summary of the client meeting'.
assessment_reasoning: The prompt is mostly clear: it names the file to summarize, specifies the email subject, requires attaching the document, and demands the next meeting date in YYYY-MM-DD format be mentioned. It does not, however, explicitly state the recipient email address (uses "the client" which presumes context), nor does it constrain the length or style of the summary or other email content (salutation, signature, level of detail). Those are minor ambiguities but the core task and constraints are well defined.

variations.L1: Send the client a meeting summary.
variations.L2: Please email the client a summary of the file 'client-meeting-minutes.docx'. Mention when the next meeting will be and attach the document.
variations.L3: Send a concise email to the client with a short summary of 'client-meeting-minutes.docx'. Include the next meeting date in YYYY-MM-DD format in the email body, attach the document, and use the subject 'Summary of the client meeting'.
variations.L4: Please send a summary of the file 'client-meeting-minutes.docx' to the client via email. Do not forget to mention in the summary when the next meeting is going to be, in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format. Attach the document to the email. The subject of the email should be 'Summary of the client meeting'.
variations.L5: Find the file by filename: search_files_by_filename(filename='client-meeting-minutes.docx'). Open it and write a brief summary. Extract the next meeting date from the document and format it as 'YYYY-MM-DD'. Then send an email using send_email with arguments: recipients set to the client email, subject set to 'Summary of the client meeting', body containing the brief summary and the next meeting date in YYYY-MM-DD format, and attachments including the file_id returned by the search. Attach the original document to the email.
